transitions.note: Peer review by GPT-5.2 (via PAL MCP)
Commons-first with human gate. Core distillation modules (distill.py, tactics.py, user_model.py) liv

question: What is the right architecture for a tactical intelligence layer that distills cross-session patterns from Claude Code transcripts into reusable, confidence-scored tactics, anti-patterns, and user behavioral models — and how should it integrate with claude-session-commons?

correctness.description: Does the architecture produce accurate, non-garbage tactics? Can it avoid the -3% AGENTS.md failure mode?

implementation_effort.description: How much new code, how many repos touched, how long to MVP?

self_improvement.description: Can the system improve its own distillation, retrieval, and thresholds over time?

safety.description: Resistance to garbage tactics, prompt injection, and harmful auto-generation. Human gate integrity.

ecosystem_fit.description: How well does it integrate with commons, resume-resume, the daemon, and the existing Eidos toolchain?

context: eidos-myelin is a new tool in the Eidos AGI ecosystem. It sits on top of claude-session-commons (published PyPI library that parses Claude Code session JSONL transcripts, chunks them, embeds with fastembed/sqlite-vec, classifies, and has analyze_patterns() for per-session pattern extraction). The gap: commons captures everything but nothing turns it into wisdom. Every new session starts from scratch. eidos-myelin should distill cross-session patterns into three kinds of learned knowledge: (1) Tactics — what consistently works, promoted after repeated observation, confidence-scored with decay; (2) Anti-patterns — what to stop doing, from high-tool-call/low-progress sessions and user corrections; (3) User Model — behavioral profile predicting productive sessions. GPT-5.2 graded the concept 7/10, identified key risks (garbage tactics from noisy data, prompt injection from LLM-generated text, need for evaluation harness), and recommended: separate package on top of commons, lifecycle states (draft→candidate→active→stale→retired), human-in-the-loop activation, provenance linking to specific sessions/turns, scoped tactics (global vs project), and a minimal V1 that only distills project-scoped operational constraints with explicit user approval. Constraints: single user, ~20 repos, ~200 sessions, local SQLite, distillation via claude -p with haiku.
